quizQuestion:
Je {___} aller nager cet après-midi.
(I would like to go swimming this afternoon.)
sentenceTranslation: I would like to go swimming this afternoon.
explanation: voudrais — je form of vouloir in the conditionnel. Stem: voudr-. Used for polite requests and wishes instead of the more abrupt je veux.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} la rencontrer un jour.
(I would like to meet her one day.)
sentenceTranslation: I would like to meet her one day.
explanation: voudrais — je form of vouloir (conditionnel). Softens the request compared to je veux, expressing a polite wish.

quizQuestion:
Tu {___} venir avec nous ce soir ?
(Would you like to come with us tonight?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you like to come with us tonight?
explanation: voudrais — tu form of vouloir (conditionnel). Identical in spelling to je voudrais; context determines person. Used to politely invite.

quizQuestion:
Il {___} un café, s'il vous plaît.
(He would like a coffee, please.)
sentenceTranslation: He would like a coffee, please.
explanation: voudrait — il/elle form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -ait. Common in restaurant or polite service contexts.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} réserver une table pour deux personnes.
(We would like to book a table for two.)
sentenceTranslation: We would like to book a table for two.
explanation: voudrions — nous form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -ions. Used for collective polite requests.

quizQuestion:
Vous {___} quelque chose à boire ?
(Would you like something to drink?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you like something to drink?
explanation: voudriez — vous form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -iez. Very common in service and hospitality French.

quizQuestion:
Ils {___} visiter le musée demain.
(They would like to visit the museum tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: They would like to visit the museum tomorrow.
explanation: voudraient — ils/elles form of vouloir (conditionnel). Stem voudr- + -aient. Expresses a group's polite wish.

quizQuestion:
Je {___} vous aider si vous voulez.
(I could help you if you like.)
sentenceTranslation: I could help you if you like.
explanation: pourrais — je form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ais. Used to offer help or make polite requests: 'Est-ce que je pourrais…?'

quizQuestion:
Tu {___} fermer la porte, s'il te plaît ?
(Could you close the door, please?)
sentenceTranslation: Could you close the door, please?
explanation: pourrais — tu form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Identical spelling to je pourrais. Polite alternative to 'Ferme la porte !'

quizQuestion:
Il {___} partir plus tôt si nécessaire.
(He could leave earlier if necessary.)
sentenceTranslation: He could leave earlier if necessary.
explanation: pourrait — il/elle form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ait. Expresses possibility or polite permission.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} se retrouver au café demain matin.
(We could meet at the café tomorrow morning.)
sentenceTranslation: We could meet at the café tomorrow morning.
explanation: pourrions — nous form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -ions. Used to suggest possibilities politely.

quizQuestion:
Vous {___} répéter la question, s'il vous plaît ?
(Could you repeat the question, please?)
sentenceTranslation: Could you repeat the question, please?
explanation: pourriez — vous form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -iez. Very common polite request formula in formal French.

quizQuestion:
Ils {___} organiser la réunion la semaine prochaine.
(They could organise the meeting next week.)
sentenceTranslation: They could organise the meeting next week.
explanation: pourraient — ils/elles form of pouvoir (conditionnel). Stem pourr- + -aient. Expresses a polite collective possibility.

quizQuestion:
J'{___} être guitariste un jour.
(I would like to be a guitarist one day.)
sentenceTranslation: I would like to be a guitarist one day.
explanation: aimerais — je form of aimer (conditionnel). Regular verb: stem aimer- + -ais. Expresses a personal wish or aspiration.

quizQuestion:
J'{___} aller à Londres pour les vacances.
(I would like to go to London for the holidays.)
sentenceTranslation: I would like to go to London for the holidays.
explanation: aimerais — je form of aimer (conditionnel). Softer and more wishful than je veux; common for travel plans and personal desires.

quizQuestion:
Il {___} partir tôt pour éviter les embouteillages.
(He would like to leave early to avoid traffic.)
sentenceTranslation: He would like to leave early to avoid traffic.
explanation: aimerait — il/elle form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -ait. Expresses a third-person wish or preference politely.

quizQuestion:
Nous {___} rester un peu plus longtemps.
(We would like to stay a little longer.)
sentenceTranslation: We would like to stay a little longer.
explanation: aimerions — nous form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -ions. Used to express a collective wish politely.

quizQuestion:
Vous {___} regarder la télévision ce soir ?
(Would you like to watch TV this evening?)
sentenceTranslation: Would you like to watch TV this evening?
explanation: aimeriez — vous form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -iez. A polite way to inquire about someone's preferences.

quizQuestion:
Ils {___} visiter la tour Eiffel pendant leur séjour.
(They would like to visit the Eiffel Tower during their stay.)
sentenceTranslation: They would like to visit the Eiffel Tower during their stay.
explanation: aimeraient — ils/elles form of aimer (conditionnel). Stem aimer- + -aient. Expresses a third-person plural wish.

quizQuestion:
Complete the real condition sentence: Si tu {___}, je viens.
(If you want, I'll come.)
sentenceTranslation: If you want, I'll come.
explanation: Si + présent expresses a real, achievable condition. 'Si tu veux' (if you want) is followed by a présent or futur: je viens or je viendrai. NOT conditionnel after si.

correctAnswer: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai.
acceptableAlternatives: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai
quizQuestion:
Which sentence correctly uses si + présent for a real condition?
(A) Si j'aurai le temps, je téléphone.
(B) Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai.
(C) Si j'aurais le temps, je téléphonerai.
(D) Si j'avais le temps, je téléphonerai.
sentenceTranslation: If I have time, I will call.
explanation: Si j'ai le temps, je téléphonerai — si is followed by présent (ai), and the result clause uses futur simple (téléphonerai). Never use futur or conditionnel directly after si.

correctAnswer: Si il fait beau, je sortirai.
quizQuestion:
Complétez avec la bonne structure : Si il {___} beau, je sortirai.
(If the weather is nice, I will go out.)
sentenceTranslation: If the weather is nice, I will go out.
explanation: Si il fait beau — si + présent (fait) introduces a real condition. Result clause uses futur simple (sortirai). Note: spoken French often contracts to S'il.

correctAnswer: Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi.
acceptableAlternatives: Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi
quizQuestion:
Which correctly combines si + présent with an imperatif result?
(A) Si vous aurez des questions, appelez-moi.
(B) Si vous avez des questions, appelez-moi.
(C) Si vous aviez des questions, appelez-moi.
(D) Si vous avez des questions, vous appeliez.
sentenceTranslation: If you have any questions, call me.
explanation: Si vous avez des questions — si + présent can be followed by an impératif in the result clause. Here: si + avez (présent) → appelez-moi (impératif).

quizQuestion:
Complete the real condition: Si je {___}, j'irai.
(If I can, I will go.)
sentenceTranslation: If I can, I will go.
explanation: Si je peux — pouvoir (présent) after si. Result clause: futur simple (j'irai). This short pattern is extremely common in everyday French.

correctAnswer: Si elle arrive tôt, nous partirons.
acceptableAlternatives: Si elle arrive tôt, nous partirons
quizQuestion:
Complétez : Si elle {___} tôt, nous partirons.
(If she arrives early, we will leave.)
sentenceTranslation: If she arrives early, we will leave.
explanation: Si elle arrive — arrive is présent (not futur or conditionnel). Result clause uses futur simple: partirons. A hallmark A2 real-condition pattern.

quizQuestion:
Quelle phrase utilise correctement si + présent + impératif ?
(A) Si tu seras fatigué, repose-toi.
(B) Si tu étais fatigué, repose-toi.
(C) Si tu es fatigué, repose-toi.
(D) Si tu sois fatigué, repose-toi.
sentenceTranslation: If you are tired, rest.
explanation: Si tu es fatigué — être in présent (es) after si. Result: impératif (repose-toi). Never use futur/conditionnel/subjonctif directly after si in real conditions.
